title: The Magic of Slime School
summary: Students gather at Slime School to create vibrant, gooey masterpieces under the watchful eye of the Slime Teacher. From silver creatures brought to life with magic spells to pink mice holding chocolate cakes, the classroom is filled with colorful imagination. After a busy morning of crafting and sharing their slime sculptures, the students spend the rest of the day bouncing through the sticky hills of Slime World before returning home with their families.

highlightQuote.text: Yeah, but what about an apron to keep my other apron dry?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right.
Dad, I got a super big boogey at school.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And then what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I was getting my boogies with Miss Susan.
Oh, did she help you?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No, I did it all by myself.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Where would you like to sit for Stars and Stories?
Right in your lap.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh good, okay, let's go right here.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I like staying up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes. Now...
Let's tell more of the Slime World.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: More of the Slime World? So where were we in the Slime World?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: School Slime World.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh Slime School, that's right. But what were they going to learn at Slime School?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: First they were learning how to make printing with me, and now they're going to do the work there. Teacher, what work is there at Slime School?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, we have some new students to welcome today to Slime School. What are your names?
Seeker and Uncle Bill.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Welcome Seeker, welcome Uncle Bill. Now have you done slime before, either of you?
Nope.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh you've never done slime before. Welcome to Slime School! You're going to make your own slime now. Do you know what ingredients you need?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No. Wait dad, but I do know one ingredient. Baking soda, glue, and food coloring. That's all we need for now, but yes, we also need the jar of like...
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh it's the contact lens solution, says Slime Teacher. Now you have your ingredients. So Seeker and Uncle Bill, I'll give you each your own bowl. Now mix them up. Here's everything you need.
Sophie says, I'm used to baking.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh hi Sophie, have you made slime before?
No.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Would you like to try?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I don't want to get my apron sticky.

Oh I can understand that. Hmm. What could we do to help you not get sticky? Maybe you could wear some gloves so you don't get it on your hands and you could use tongs to pick up the slime and a spoon to stir it. And then you won't get sticky. How does that sound?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, but what about an apron to keep my other apron dry?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: You want to put an apron over your other apron? That's a great idea! We have plenty of apron-aprons here. It's just like an apron, but it's an apron designed to go over your apron. Here's one, there you go. Does that fit okay?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, it's just a little bit tight.
Okay you can loosen it a little bit. Now Sophie, Seeker and Uncle Bill here are making their own slime for the very first time, so you can ask them for help.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Hey, hey, I just need two spoons. One for the baking soda, one for the contact solution. Okay, here we go. And then just squirt out all the glue.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Squirt squirt squirt.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And now just dump, dump it into the bowl. Uncle Bill, would you help me dump the glue please?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh sure, I can give you a hand. I just don't mind and it worked out great. Okay, here you go. Dump, dump, dump. Oh Seeker, what are you doing? And Seeker said, Oh, I was just experimenting a little bit because I wanted to see what would happen if I tipped my bowl upside down a little bit and dripped out. But I just scooped it back in. I made a little tiny bit of a mess. Sorry, everybody. And Slime Teacher said, that's okay Seeker, here's a wet wipe. Oh yeah, okay, I'll clean that up. And then what did they do next?

They added in the contact solution. They poured it into the spoon and they dumped it in and then the baking soda and then the food coloring. Everyone added... So Sophie added pink, Uncle Bill added purple, and silver for Seeker. And then Isabella asked, Can I make some slime with you?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Yes of course, come join in.
And then she did what everyone else did and then she added in gold.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Gold! Oh, you're making gold slime, said the Slime Teacher. That's a very interesting idea. I like it. Now how's the stirring going everyone? And Seeker went, stir stir stir, this is hard work. And Uncle Bill went, stir stir stir stir.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And Sophie went stir stir stir, and then she showed it to the teacher.
Oh that's looking good, I think you need a little more stirring Sophie. A little sticky looking still.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Stir stir stir.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: But you've got very good color on your slime. Now...
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Izzy's mom was there too, but she was just there to help mix.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay. She's very good at mixing, Izzy's mama. And she went mix mix mix mix mix. Does anyone else need any help? And Seeker said, I think mine's just fine. And Uncle Bill said, yeah I could use a hand. My arm's getting tired. And Izzy's mom said, okay I'll help a little bit. Stir stir stir. Okay, here you go. And then pretty soon everybody had beautiful bowls full of different colored slime.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now look at my colors. I've got pink, purple, yellow, red, green, blue.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, Wow, how did you get so many colors, Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I have lots of colors of food coloring at home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. Now, said Slime Teacher, that was our work for today, making the slime. And now you get to do free play with your slime. You can do anything with it that you choose.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Squish and then Isabella squished it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Squish squish squish.
And then Isabella dumped it, rolled it, cut it, and then did crocodile teeth and then put it in a Ziploc bag and showed it to the teacher.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Wow, are you done already Isabella? Wow, you did a lot of beautiful slime work today. Congratulations.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Is it circle time or snack time or what?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Almost. Let's go and do a little sharing session so we can see what each other person did in their slime work.
So and then Isabella was the first one to show off.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh okay Isabella, your turn.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: So Isabella said, Hello everyone, my name's Isabella and um, I did a stamp and then rolled it and then did crocodile teeth. No and then I did knife kind and then I did crocodile teeth.
Wow, said everyone.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And then she shook it up in a cup.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: I see. And Uncle Bill said, How did you make crocodile teeth out of your slime?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just... and then Isabella pulled out something really sharp, which was a little flower-like thing with triangular-shaped teeth.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh a little roller, said Uncle Bill. And that's how you made the teeth on your slime?
Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: What a cool idea!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Isabella said, I Ziploc bagged it like this.
Oh. And Uncle Bill said, Hey can I be next to share?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Slime Teacher said, Okay. And Uncle Bill said, My name is Uncle Bill and I made some beautiful slime. His was purple. I made some purple slime and I decided to try to make a slime house out of mine, but it was kind of hard because it kept flopping over and just turning into a blob. And so, instead of trying to build a slime house, I decided to make a row of slime balls and that worked really well. And then I connected them into a circle and I made a circle of slime balls. And I realized if I had more slime I could maybe make a kind of igloo out of slime but I didn't have time. And that was a rhyme, hey. And Seeker said, Me me me, can I go next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Sophie said, Can I go third after Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, Well I decided to make a slime creature. And so I made a little silver ball for a head and then a bigger ball for a body and then I stretched out the slime and made little slime snakes and I attached them to the body to make arms and legs. And then I tried to stand my creature up but it flopped over and went k-chk and turned into a little blob. And I wanted to bring my slime creature to life and so I cast a little spell. Wake up slime, it's your time. Dance slime, dance. Dance slime, dance. And then my spell worked and the little slime creature went wubble wubble wubble and it was dancing so fast and it got so excited that it ran right off the edge of the table and splat on the floor. So then I had to clean it up.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then it was Sophie's turn. And Sophie said, I made a mouse named Wenden out of slime.
Oh.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: A slime mouse. That's a very interesting idea, said the Slime Teacher.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm holding a chocolate cake.
A slime mouse holding a chocolate cake. Incredible! Well thank you everyone for sharing. You all made wonderful slime projects for Slime School today.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And Izzy said, um, I already did mine.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yes. Thank you for sharing everyone. That's the end of our slime lesson for today.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And then was it time for circle?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: It was time for snack, they ate that. Then they had circle, then they went and had lunch. And then they had nap, and then... it was circle time and then work time and then more snack time and then outside time all day.
Wow. Now they had outside time all day but it was in Slime World so they were running outside in the slime. Hehe.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No they were bouncing.

Bouncing in the slime and squishing around. Squish squish squish. And it was sticky and gooey, haha. And but a nice thing about running around in the slime is if you trip and fall you go splat right in the slime so you don't get hurt. Um and you could make slime sculptures and wrap the slime around yourself if you want to but then you get sticky. But then you take it off but then you could just take a little slime bath. Wubble wubble wubble. And so everyone got all tired out from running around in Slime World.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And then everyone's moms came, but Isabella's dad came and her mom and then her little sister Ava too.
Oh yes.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And then they all went home together.
And that's the end of Stars and Stories.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Can you turn off the light?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes. And now it's time to...

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Uncle Bill
characters.description: A diligent crew member and master Lego architect, this former visitor to Balloon World crafts perfectly symmetrical stairways and shapes iridescent purple slime into glistening rocket ships. Renowned for a sharp eye that once pierced Pluto’s crystalline shadows, they recently turned their gaze toward the forest’s depths, acting as a compassionate friend to discover and tend to a sick creature hidden in the brush. Whether providing blocks for builders, weaving bedtime stories, or offering woodland mercy, they remain a helpful soul. Yet, between these moments of structural precision and quiet rescue, they harbor a penchant for deep rest, often found napping peacefully inside the colorful confines of a toy container, tucked away amidst the playthings they share.

characters.name: Slime Teacher
characters.description: The encouraging instructor at Slime School.

characters.name: Sophie
characters.description: The resilient owner and artisan of the Eat and Greet Bakery is a vibrant pink mouse with an iconic, rounded silhouette, typically found in a pink dress and a flour-dusted blue apron. A master of alchemical confections, she kneads life into glowing pink slime alongside her loyal companion, Wenden—a slime mouse who studies the science of her color-mixing. Her delicate appearance belies a legendary endurance, famously proven when she carried her brother Winston through the freezing wilds to the gates of Snow World.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Izzy's Mom
characters.description: A character in the story who helps the students mix their slime.

characters.name: Dad
characters.description: Kitty’s father remains a pillar of steady eccentricity, an unflappable co-pilot and accommodating listener who balances technical logistics with a patient support for his daughter’s restless need for activity. He manages mission equipment with the same meticulous intensity he once brought to scanning the midnight undergrowth for missing cats, his stoicism famously underscored by the irony of a nagging knee complaint—diagnosed as overgrown toenails—that never wavers his resolve.

characters.name: Ava
characters.description: Isabella's little sister who comes along for school pickup.

characters.name: Wenden
characters.description: A mouse made of slime that carries a chocolate cake.

characters.name: The Slime Creature
characters.description: A silver slime sculpture with snake-like limbs that dances after a magic spell.

places.name: Slime World
places.description: A vibrant, magical realm where the landscape is sculpted from iridescent orange and blue slime. This viscous dimension is inherently bouncy and sticky, ensuring that every fall is met with a soft, rhythmic rebound and turning the undulating ground into a playground of safe, gravity-defying leaps.

places.name: Slime School
places.description: The classroom where students learn to mix ingredients and create slime sculptures.

places.name: The Outside
places.description: A gooey playground in Slime World where students bounce and run through the slime.
